author: @ClownWhisper
text:
HEY! several hundred milliamps is NOT half bad for what you have there Robert!!!!
That is actually very impressive! 

Question:  have you seen any degradation of the chlorophyll over time ?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: not yet - but i probably will

author: @fetchitnowuk
text: This might sound really stupid but does chlorofil differ from plant to plant? The reason I ask is if a plant in a colder climate is more efficient than say something in a warmer one it could be possible to find a better converter of electrical energy based on the type of plant used. Essentially nature has had several million years of tinker time to fine tune their energy conversions so what better place to steal, sorry borrow, some tips.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's good thinking mate and would form a whole load of experiments on optimizing the cell - we are not quite at that stage yet - there are other things we can do to change the readings - we have no real idea about concentrations yet
